blocks.content: He came home to the empty house.
He stood in the doorway for a long time without turning on the lights.
The lamp she loved. The mismatched mugs. Harold on the shelf in the bedroom, visible through the open door.

He stood there and looked at the one cup. Then he opened the cabinet and got the second one and set it on the counter beside the first, because he couldn't not. Because fifteen years is fifteen years and the body doesn't learn the new thing overnight. Because some mornings the muscle memory would win and both cups would be on the counter before his mind caught up to why.
He drank both cups standing at the window, looking at the yard in the dark.
